summary: I build the service that grades SOC 2 audit evidence so a human doesn't have to read every artifact.
details: Engineered an AI-driven FastAPI/Docker microservice that evaluates SOC 2 audit evidence, using semantic matching, hybrid scoring, and gated LLM adjudication to automate audit review.
Delivered 100% accuracy on 88 test artifacts with sub-3s latency, enabling real-time feedback and cutting costs.

summary: Built a tool that turns plain English questions into production SQL, for staff who don't write SQL.
details: Architected a RAG-powered natural-language-to-SQL interface that converted business questions into production-ready queries and results, reducing data request turnaround by 50% for non-technical users.
Engineered a multi-cloud data lakehouse architecture using Apache Iceberg, automating Snowflake exports directly to AWS S3 and integrating with Oracle Autonomous Database.

summary: First internship. ETL pipelines and a funding dashboard that 10+ Texas school districts use.
details: Created an API-to-Oracle Cloud ETL pipeline using PL/SQL, reducing processing time by 70%.
Built a full-stack attendance app serving 10+ school districts using a PL/SQL logic engine for funding eligibility.
Engineered an Oracle APEX funding projection dashboard using JavaScript and SQL for 10+ school districts.

summary: Turned river sensor data into a real pipeline instead of hand-edited CSVs.
details: Architected a Python/PostgreSQL pipeline replacing manual CSV workflows, increasing processing throughput by 95%.
Implemented automated integrity validations and real-time outlier alerting for high-fidelity telemetry reporting.

summary: Ran the fraternity for a year: 40% membership growth on a $10K+ annual budget.
details: Spearheaded 40% membership growth and managed a $10K+ annual operating budget.
Directed philanthropy and technical marketing initiatives, earning recognition in the Gastric Cancer Foundation magazine.
